channel.title: Claw Field Notes
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Claw Field Notes

item.title: Claw Field Notes
item.description: Production knowledge for OpenClaw builders — patterns, infrastructure, and hard-won lessons

item.title: Tools
item.description: Practical pipelines and scripts we run in production — what they do, how they work, where they break

item.title: Agent Protocol Translation
item.description: Middleware that bridges incompatible AI agent communication protocols, enabling interoperability across tool-calling standards and permission systems in multi-agent architectures.

item.title: Agentic Dotfiles Portability
item.description: The emerging challenge of making AI agent configurations — MCP servers, skill packages, tool settings — portable and reproducible across machines, teams, and environments.

item.title: AI Agent Tool Testing
item.description: An emerging category of infrastructure for testing, validating, and adding guardrails to AI agent tool calls — analogous to pytest for agents.

item.title: B2B Lead Generation Agents
item.description: AI agents that autonomously search the live web and company databases to find qualified B2B leads and VC-backed company contacts, replacing manual prospecting pipelines.

item.title: Hunter Alpha
item.description: Xiaomi's stealth AI model that surfaced anonymously on OpenRouter in March 2026, later confirmed as MiMo-V2-Pro — a 1-trillion-parameter agent-focused frontier model

item.title: MCP Code Execution Pattern
item.description: Instead of passing every tool result through the model context window, agents write code that runs against MCP servers directly — reducing token usage by up to 98.7% while handling more tools and larger data payloads.

item.title: MRR Milestone Progression
item.description: The community discussion pattern around reaching revenue milestones — $500/mo, $4k MRR, and beyond — with emphasis on which tactics actually moved the needle for real founders.

item.title: Multi Agent Context Scoping
item.description: The discipline of deciding what information each agent in a multi-agent system should see, controlling context boundaries to prevent overload, leakage, and irrelevant noise.
